questions.question: Anika - Journal, Petty Cash and Ledger

parts.text: Prepare the journal entry to record the assets, liabilities and capital of Anika on 1 March 2025.
parts.answer: Debit: Premises $90,000, Motor vehicles $14,500, Inventory $3,625, Trade receivables – Kofi $3,000, Davia $2,140, Petty cash $86. Credit: Bank $1,080, Trade payables – Ado $1,925, Sam $210, Capital $110,136.

parts.text: Complete Anika's petty cash book on page 3. Balance the petty cash book and bring down the balance on 1 April 2025.
parts.answer: Balance b/d $86, Bank $64, Total received $150. Payments: Petrol $30, Computer paper $12, Sam $48, Postage $6, Car cleaning $8. Total paid $104, Balance c/d $46. Balance b/d Apr 1 $46, Bank $104.

parts.text: Prepare Sam's account in the purchases ledger. Balance the account and bring down the balance on 1 April 2025.
parts.answer: Mar 17 Petty cash $48, Mar 25 Purchases returns $57, Balance c/d $219. Total $324. Mar 1 Balance b/d $210, Mar 21 Purchases $114. Balance b/d Apr 1 $219.

questions.question: Mo - Depreciation, Disposal and Rental Income

parts.text: Calculate the total depreciation which has been charged on the delivery vehicle over the three years.
Prepare the disposal of motor vehicles account for the year ended 31 December 2024.
parts.answer: Debit: Motor vehicles $10,000. Credit: Provision for depreciation $4,880, Bank $2,900, Income statement $2,220.

parts.text: Calculate the cost of the new vehicle which should be recorded in the non-current assets account.
parts.answer: Cost of vehicle $12,500 + Number plates $215 = $12,715

parts.text: Advise Mo whether or not he should open a shop on his farm. Justify your answer with advantages and disadvantages.
parts.answer: Advantages: No motor expenses/delivery costs, can utilize time on other aspects, might increase sales/customers/profits, could sell vehicle. Disadvantages: Cost of converting building, cost of running shop, customers may not travel/prefer delivery, new van just purchased. Recommendation required.

parts.text: Prepare the rental income account for the year ended 31 December 2024.
parts.answer: Jan 1 Balance b/d $160, Dec 31 Income statement $960 (12 × $80). Mar 1 Bank $320, Sep 1 Bank $720, Dec 31 Balance c/d $80. Balance b/d Jan 1 $80.

parts.text: Give one possible reason why Mo has a higher gross profit margin than Barry.
parts.answer: They may sell different types of produce / Mo has a higher selling price than Barry / Mo's cost of producing/cost of sales is lower than Barry's

parts.text: Give one possible reason why Mo has a higher net profit margin than Barry.
parts.answer: Barry has to pay rent whereas Mo does not / Mo has lower expenses/controls expenses better / Using different accounting methods e.g. different depreciation methods

questions.question: Nabil - Error Correction and Bank Reconciliation

parts.text: Prepare the journal entries to correct errors 1–5. Narratives are not required.
parts.answer: 1. Wages Dr $425, Purchases Cr $425. 2. Discount allowed Dr $38, Suspense Cr $38. 3. Suspense Dr $100, Sales Cr $100. 4. Purchases Dr $170, Bank Cr $170. 5. Insurance Dr $210, Suspense Cr $210, Bank Dr $420.

parts.text: Prepare Nabil's suspense account for April 2025.
parts.answer: Apr 30 Sales $100, Bank $210. Apr 30 Difference on trial balance $272, Discount allowed $38. Total $310.

parts.text: Calculate the corrected cash at bank balance on 30 April 2025.
parts.answer: Original cash at bank balance $935 + Bank charges $90 = $1,025. Less: Purchases $170, Insurance $420 = $590. Corrected balance = $435.

parts.text: State the purpose of a bank statement.
parts.answer: To enable the customer to compare their records against those of the bank / To check the bank balance in the cash book against that shown in the bank's records / To identify the reason for any differences / To discover errors/omissions / To reconcile the bank statement balance with that in the cash book

parts.text: Prepare the Statement of Changes in Equity for H Limited for the year ended 30 April 2025.
parts.answer: Opening balances: Share capital $120,000, General reserve $20,000, Retained earnings $33,635. Profit for year $26,700, Transfer to general reserve $5,000, Dividends paid $5,340. Closing: Share capital $120,000, General reserve $25,000, Retained earnings $49,995.

parts.text: Prepare the Statement of Financial Position for H Limited as at 30 April 2025.
parts.answer: Non-current assets: Fixtures and equipment $155,000, Motor vehicles $16,875. Current assets: Inventory $28,120, Trade receivables $33,000 less Provision $990 = $32,010. Total assets $232,005. Equity: Share capital $120,000, General reserve $25,000, Retained earnings $49,995. Non-current liabilities: 5% Debentures $5,000. Current liabilities: Trade payables $26,815, Bank overdraft $5,195. Total $232,005.

parts.text: Define capital employed.
parts.answer: The total funds provided by the owners of a business / The difference between the assets and liabilities of a business

parts.text: Calculate the return on capital employed (ROCE) for H Limited.
parts.answer: ROCE = Profit before interest and tax / Capital employed × 100 = 26,700 + 250 / 194,995 + 5,000 × 100 = 26,950 / 199,995 × 100 = 13.48%

parts.text: Advise H Limited whether or not they should issue $25,000 of debentures. Justify your answer with advantages and disadvantages.
parts.answer: Points for: No liability once repaid / Will not reduce shareholders' stake / Low amount of loans at present / Funds available quickly. Points against: Interest must be paid / May be secured against assets / Have to be repaid / Already have commitment to repay existing debentures. Recommendation required.

parts.text: Prepare the Manufacturing Account for Grace for the year ended 31 March 2025.
parts.answer: Cost of material consumed: Opening inventory $5,345 + Purchases $72,870 + Carriage inwards $1,220 = $79,435 - Closing inventory $7,100 = $72,335. Direct wages $29,175. Prime cost $101,510. Factory overheads: Supervisor wages $26,000, Factory power $15,435, Rent and insurance $7,800, Depreciation $23,040. Total factory overheads $72,275. Cost of production $173,215.

parts.text: Calculate the value of the closing inventory of finished goods on 31 March 2025.
parts.answer: 237 units × $14 = $3,318. 3 units at NRV = $39. 15 units at cost $14 = $210. Total = $3,567.

parts.text: Explain the accounting principle being applied when factory equipment is recorded at its original cost.
parts.answer: All assets and expenses are recorded at their actual cost / Factory equipment is recorded at the amount of its actual/original cost

parts.text: Advise Grace whether or not she should produce handbags. Justify your answer with advantages and disadvantages.
parts.answer: Points for: Not dependent on suppliers for price/quality/reliability, possibility of higher sales/customers/profit. Points against: New equipment may be required, may be cheaper to purchase, may produce inferior quality, may not meet demand, may need additional space, cost of production will increase. Recommendation required.
